skill.name: apple-mail
skill.description: Guide for using AppleScript and osascript to automate Apple Mail tasks such as reading messages, sending replies, and managing mailboxes.
skill.summary: Automate Apple Mail with AppleScript examples for reading, searching, sending, replying, moving, and deleting messages.
skill.author: BfdCampos

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified extensive external command usage because the skill is built around osascript examples for Apple Mail automation. I found no evidence of malicious network exfiltration, prompt injection, or weak cryptographic code; however, the documented commands can read private email content and perform mailbox-changing actions, so publication should include a clear privacy and automation warning.

medium_findings.title: Apple Mail Access Through External osascript Commands
medium_findings.description: The external command findings are true positives. The skill provides osascript examples that enumerate accounts, read mailbox messages, retrieve full email content, search senders and subjects, and return message metadata from Apple Mail. This is legitimate for an Apple Mail automation skill, but it exposes sensitive local email data when executed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit osascript invocations against Apple Mail and the semantic purpose is email access. I did not find evidence that the skill sends the collected data to an external service.

medium_findings.title: Examples Can Send, Move, Reply To, Or Delete Email
medium_findings.description: The skill includes examples that send new messages, send replies, move messages to another mailbox, and delete messages by criteria. It also documents visible:false as an option for background sending. These actions are expected for mail automation but can alter a user account if executed without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant AppleScript examples directly call send, move, and delete operations. The examples use placeholders and include some safety guidance, so the risk is capability-based rather than malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm blockers at lines 3, 8, and 284-287. These lines describe Apple Mail automation and mailbox destination logic; I found no cryptographic functions, hash algorithms, key handling, or encryption code in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose and Apple Mail mailbox operations, not crypto code. The match appears to come from unrelated text patterns rather than an actual algorithm use.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported system reconnaissance at lines 356, 443, and 476. These locations reference Apple Mail message identifiers, limiting message retrieval, and using specific email searches; they do not collect host, network, user, or environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is limited to Apple Mail message properties and performance guidance. I found no evidence of commands such as hostname, whoami, network enumeration, or system inventory collection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Invocation Of osascript
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill repeatedly shows bash heredocs and one-line commands that execute AppleScript through osascript. This is necessary for the skill purpose, but it is still external command execution with access to a local macOS application and private user data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The osascript invocations are direct and repeated across the documented workflows. The risk depends on user consent and execution context, not hidden code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive Or Silent Mail Actions
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples include direct send, delete, and move operations, and document background sending with visible:false. These actions should require explicit user confirmation before execution in any agent workflow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The operations are clearly present and can modify mailbox state. The skill is transparent about these actions and does not hide them.

content.value_statement: Apple Mail automation is hard because account and mailbox names vary across providers. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code tested AppleScript patterns for reading, searching, sending, replying, and managing messages.
actual_capabilities: Lists Apple Mail accounts and mailbox names through AppleScript examples.
Reads recent message metadata and full message content from selected mailboxes.
Searches messages by subject, sender, unread state, and mailbox location.
Creates new emails and threaded replies using Apple Mail commands.
Moves or deletes messages that match explicit criteria.
Explains common AppleScript errors for Apple Mail account and mailbox access.
limitations: Requires macOS with Apple Mail installed and configured.
Requires local automation permissions before Mail data can be accessed.
Examples use placeholders and must be adapted to real account and mailbox names.
Unsafe prompts can send or delete mail if executed without review.

use_cases.title: Triage Recent Mail
use_cases.description: List recent messages, unread counts, and sender details before deciding what needs attention.

use_cases.title: Build Local Email Workflows
use_cases.description: Use repeatable AppleScript patterns for account lookup, mailbox traversal, search filters, and message actions.

use_cases.title: Draft And Send Responses
use_cases.description: Create new messages or threaded replies from Apple Mail while preserving subject and recipient context.

prompt_templates.title: List Mail Accounts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the apple-mail skill to show the safest AppleScript command for listing Apple Mail accounts. Explain what permission prompt I may see.

prompt_templates.title: Read Recent Messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the apple-mail skill to read the five most recent inbox message subjects and senders for account <account name>. Do not read full bodies.

prompt_templates.title: Find Messages By Sender
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the apple-mail skill to search account <account name> for inbox messages from <sender>. Return subject, date, and mailbox only.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare A Threaded Reply
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the apple-mail skill to draft a threaded reply to the newest message from <sender>. Keep the compose window visible for review before sending.

output_examples.input: List my Apple Mail accounts before running a mailbox query.
output_examples.output: The assistant provides a short osascript command that returns account names and notes that macOS may request Mail automation permission.

output_examples.input: Find unread messages in my work inbox without loading message bodies.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches the target inbox, returns a count plus subject and sender lines, and avoids full content extraction.

output_examples.input: Reply to the latest message from a vendor and keep the thread intact.
output_examples.output: The assistant uses the Apple Mail reply command, prepares the response text, and keeps the compose window visible for approval.

best_practices: List accounts and mailbox names before running read, move, send, or delete operations.
Use visible compose windows and explicit confirmation for any outgoing message.
Limit searches by account, mailbox, sender, subject, or count to reduce exposure of private mail.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad full-content mailbox reads when metadata is enough.
Do not use visible:false for sending unless the user explicitly approved the exact message.
Do not delete or move messages using vague criteria such as broad subject keywords.

faq.question: Does this skill access email directly?
faq.answer: It provides AppleScript examples that can access Apple Mail when executed on macOS with permission.

faq.question: Can it send email?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes examples for new messages and threaded replies, so review prompts carefully before sending.

faq.question: Does it work outside macOS?
faq.answer: No. The examples depend on Apple Mail, AppleScript, and osascript on macOS.

faq.question: Why does it iterate through mailboxes?
faq.answer: Apple Mail account mailbox names vary, so iterating avoids common errors with direct inbox access.

faq.question: Can it read full message bodies?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for full content reads, but metadata-only reads are safer and faster.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it uses external commands and can modify local mail.
